name: Classic 3m rate differential carry
rationale: Higher-yielding currencies have historically delivered excess returns versus lower-yielding ones in G10, compensating crash risk. 40+ year documented anomaly.

name: Carry-to-volatility ratio
rationale: Risk-adjusted carry outperforms naive carry by avoiding overweight in high-vol pairs that crash hardest

name: Real interest rate carry
rationale: Nominal carry confounds inflation differentials. Real rates better capture true compensation for risk.

name: VIX-filtered carry
rationale: Carry trades crash during funding-liquidity events. Closing positions when VIX > 30 avoids the worst drawdowns.

name: Term-spread carry
rationale: Steeper yield curves predict currency strength; carry signal augmented with 2s10s differential

name: Triple-swap Wednesday timing
rationale: Forex convention pays triple swap on Wednesday rollover; entering long-carry Tuesday evening captures the bump

name: Momentum-confirmed carry
rationale: Trade carry only when 6m momentum confirms; reduces tail risk by avoiding 'falling knife' carry trades

name: Carry minus dollar
rationale: Decompose carry into common dollar factor and idiosyncratic carry; latter is cleaner edge

name: Forward implied carry
rationale: Forward points imply expected carry differently than spot rate differentials; uses market-priced expectations
fails_in: covered interest parity arbitrage closes most gaps

name: AUDJPY-specific carry
rationale: AUDJPY is the canonical retail carry trade; isolated test of the cleanest carry pair

name: Yen-cross unwind detector
rationale: Sharp moves in USDJPY often signal carry unwind; close all long-carry positions when threshold breached

name: Carry with regime conditioning
rationale: Carry works in stable regimes, fails in crisis; condition on regime classifier
expected_direction: higher Sharpe via avoiding crisis periods
fails_in: regime classifier lag at turning points

name: 1-month time-series momentum
rationale: Documented persistence in 1-12m past returns across 58 assets including FX. Behavioral underreaction.

name: 3-month TSMOM
rationale: 3m horizon is the sweet spot in the original paper; balances signal quality and turnover

name: 6-month TSMOM
rationale: Longer horizon reduces noise but slower to detect regime shifts

name: 12-month TSMOM
rationale: Original signal in equity literature; tests if FX has similar persistence at 1yr

name: Cross-sectional FX momentum
rationale: Rank pairs by past return, long top quintile short bottom; FX-specific replication of equity XS momentum

name: Vol-scaled momentum
rationale: Scale position size by inverse volatility; reduces drawdowns without sacrificing return

name: Risk-adjusted momentum
rationale: Sharpe of past returns better than raw return; corrects for risk taken

name: Short-term reversal
rationale: Returns reverse at very short horizons (1-week) due to liquidity provision/overreaction

name: 52-week high breakout
rationale: Anchoring bias creates reluctance to update price near 52w highs; breakouts continue

name: Momentum with vol filter
rationale: Momentum crashes in high-vol regimes; filter out trades when vol > 75th percentile

name: Acceleration / second derivative
rationale: Change in momentum (acceleration) leads price; catches regime shifts earlier

name: Trend quality (R-squared)
rationale: Trade only when trend is statistically clean (high R-squared of linear fit)

name: 50/200 SMA crossover
rationale: Classic golden/death cross; long-only when 50d > 200d

name: Momentum + carry interaction
rationale: Trade pairs where carry AND momentum agree on direction; diversification of two factors

name: Momentum during risk-on only
rationale: FX momentum stronger in risk-on regimes; condition on VIX or equity market state

name: PPP deviation reversal
rationale: Currencies revert to purchasing power parity over multi-year horizons; the 'PPP puzzle' is slow convergence not non-convergence
fails_in: 5-15 year mean reversion is too slow

name: REER deviation
source: BIS Real Effective Exchange Rate methodology
rationale: Real effective exchange rate weighted by trade partners; deviations from 5y mean revert

name: 5-year z-score mean reversion
rationale: Extreme deviations from 5y mean tend to revert; behavioral overreaction

name: Bollinger band reversal
source: TA literature, Lo et al 2000 statistical tests
rationale: Price touching 2-sigma bands shows higher reversal probability

name: RSI extreme reversal
rationale: RSI < 30 or > 70 extreme readings show reversal tendency at short horizons

name: AUDNZD cointegration
rationale: AUD and NZD highly cointegrated as commodity currencies; deviations revert

name: EURGBP mean reversion
rationale: Closely linked European economies; spread reverts to mean

name: Big Mac PPP
rationale: Folk version of PPP with quarterly updates; extreme deviations revert

name: Low-vol effect in FX
rationale: Lower-vol assets earn higher risk-adjusted returns due to leverage constraints

name: Vol breakout continuation
rationale: Volatility expansion from compressed base often signals trend onset

name: Vol contraction to expansion
rationale: Periods of extreme low volatility precede expansion; trade direction of expansion

name: Realized vs implied vol divergence
rationale: When realized vol > implied vol, options are underpriced; for spot, signals coming realized moves

name: Garman-Klass volatility filter
rationale: OHLC-based vol estimator more efficient than close-to-close; better filter for risk
fails_in: persistent high vol with continued edge

name: Vol regime classifier
rationale: Two-state HMM on volatility; trade only in low-vol state

name: VIX as cross-asset signal
rationale: Equity vol leads FX risk-off; flat positions when VIX spikes

name: Vol-of-vol signal
rationale: Second-derivative of volatility predicts regime changes; uncertainty proxy

name: 2s10s yield curve signal
rationale: Yield curve inversion predicts recession; risk-off for high-yielders

name: Credit spread risk signal
rationale: Investment-grade credit spreads widen before risk-off in FX

name: Inflation differential momentum
rationale: Currencies with rising inflation differentials weaken; trade differentials in trend

name: Manufacturing PMI differential
rationale: Manufacturing PMI leads GDP; differential predicts currency strength

name: Trade balance momentum
rationale: Improving trade balances strengthen currencies via FX demand for exports

name: Current account differential
rationale: Persistent CA surpluses build FX appreciation pressure

name: Real GDP growth differential
rationale: Growth differentials drive long-term real exchange rate appreciation

name: Unemployment trend differential
rationale: Falling unemployment supports currency via rate hike expectations

name: M2 money supply growth
rationale: Faster money supply growth weakens currency (monetarist model)

name: Central bank balance sheet
rationale: Faster balance sheet expansion weakens currency relative to peers

name: Dollar index momentum
rationale: DXY trend regimes have strong correlation with non-USD pairs

params.filter: align position with DXY trend direction

name: 10y bond yield differential momentum
rationale: Rising long-end yields attract capital, strengthen currency

name: Government debt-to-GDP
rationale: High and rising debt-to-GDP weakens currencies long-term

name: Equity market momentum vs FX
rationale: Strong equity markets attract foreign capital, strengthen currency

params.direction: long currency of stronger equity market

fails_in: risk-off when both equity and FX flee together

name: Real rate differential momentum
rationale: Trend in real rate differentials drives currency over months
fails_in: inflation surprises that don't transmit to rates

name: AUD vs copper
rationale: AUD is a copper-export driven currency; copper price leads AUDUSD

params.direction: long AUD when copper trending up

name: CAD vs WTI oil
rationale: Canadian dollar tracks oil prices via energy exports

params.direction: long CAD when oil trending up

fails_in: oil shocks not transmitting due to hedging

name: NZD vs dairy prices
rationale: NZD is dairy-export driven; whole milk powder auctions correlate

name: AUD vs iron ore
rationale: Iron ore is Australia's largest export; price moves drive AUD

name: Commodity index momentum
rationale: Broad commodity momentum predicts commodity-currency basket

name: Oil-yen relationship
rationale: Japan is net oil importer; high oil weakens JPY via terms of trade

name: Commodity-currency basket cross
rationale: Long commodity-exporter basket vs commodity-importer basket

name: Gold-AUD relationship
rationale: Australia is major gold producer; gold price correlates with AUD
fails_in: gold rallying on risk-off (negative for AUD)

name: JPY safe-haven flows
rationale: JPY appreciates during global equity drawdowns; safe-haven
fails_in: recent BoJ policy may have weakened this

name: CHF safe-haven flows
rationale: CHF appreciates during global stress; even with SNB intervention

name: USD smile - extreme risk-off
rationale: USD strengthens in extreme risk-off due to funding/reserve currency

name: Risk-on/risk-off composite
rationale: Composite of SPX, credit spread, VIX as risk regime indicator

params.direction: long carry in risk-on, flat in risk-off

name: Real yield differential
rationale: TIPS-implied real yields drive long-term FX better than nominal

name: Bond-FX convexity
rationale: Currency moves correlate with bond market convexity at extremes

name: Gold-DXY inverse
rationale: Gold prices and USD generally inversely related

name: VIX term structure signal
rationale: VIX futures contango/backwardation signals FX risk regime

name: Equity-FX correlation regime
rationale: When equity-FX correlation flips, regime change ahead

name: Dollar funding stress indicator
rationale: Covered interest parity deviations signal dollar funding stress

name: COT extreme positioning reversal
rationale: When CFTC speculator positioning hits 2-year extreme, reversal probability rises
fails_in: persistent trends that keep building positions

name: Speculator net long extreme
rationale: Extreme speculator net long suggests crowded trade vulnerable to unwind

name: Commercial vs speculator divergence
rationale: When commercials (informed) oppose specs (sentiment), commercials win long-run

name: Spec position momentum
rationale: Positioning builds before moves; momentum in positioning predicts price

name: Open interest momentum
rationale: Rising open interest in trend direction validates trend

name: Risk reversal skew
rationale: FX option risk reversals show sentiment skew; extreme skew reverses

name: Turn of month effect
rationale: Pension/index flows around month-end create predictable FX flows

name: End-of-quarter rebalancing
rationale: Equity portfolio rebalancing creates large FX flows at quarter end

params.direction: fade quarterly equity move via FX

fails_in: declining importance as flows hedge differently

name: US Payrolls reaction
rationale: NFP surprises drive USD; standardized surprise predicts direction
fails_in: reaction faded within minutes by HFT

name: FOMC drift
rationale: Equity returns drift up before FOMC meetings; FX impact via risk-on

name: ECB rate decision drift
rationale: Similar pre-meeting drift in EUR-related pairs around ECB
fails_in: smaller and faster than FOMC effect

name: Asian session range as London signal
rationale: Asian range breakouts often direct London session moves

name: Value AND momentum
rationale: Value and momentum are negatively correlated; combining diversifies

name: Carry AND momentum agreement
rationale: Trade only when carry signal and 3m momentum agree; quality filter
fails_in: disagreement years that would have been profitable

name: Trend-aligned carry only
rationale: Add weekly trend filter to carry; avoids fighting trend

name: Regime-conditional momentum
rationale: Momentum works in trending regimes, fails in mean-reverting; condition on regime

name: Carry + vol filter + momentum
rationale: Triple filter: positive carry, momentum agreement, vol below threshold

name: Defensive carry (low-vol carry)
rationale: Carry within low-volatility pairs only; combines low-vol effect and carry
fails_in: low-vol pairs all converge to zero carry

name: Carry + value (mean reversion)
rationale: Long high-carry + undervalued, short low-carry + overvalued

name: DXY-conditional carry
rationale: Carry works best when DXY is stable/declining; flat carry during DXY breakouts

name: Vol-scaled momentum + carry
rationale: Combine vol-scaled momentum with carry; double diversification

name: Risk-off filter on all factors
rationale: Apply universal risk-off filter (vix>30 OR credit spread spike) to all strategies
fails_in: slow crises that don't trigger filters

name: PMI + carry agreement
rationale: Trade carry only when PMI differential agrees with rate differential

name: Five-factor weighted composite
rationale: Equal-weight composite: carry, momentum, value, vol, defensive
